description: Coverage baseline thresholds for the root doc-detective package, measured as the CROSS-PLATFORM UNION of the full end-to-end test suite: every OS/node matrix cell collects raw V8 coverage (NODE_V8_COVERAGE) and the coverage-merge job re-roots and reports their union via c8, so OS-gated (Windows/macOS) branches count. These values ratchet UP as coverage improves and must never be lowered casually. They MAY be re-baselined DOWNWARD only with explicit justification in the commit that lowers them — when a change adds substantial integration runtime whose full exercise necessarily lives out-of-process (e.g. the per-feature fixture matrix), so mocha's in-process V8 coverage cannot see it, after everything hermetically coverable has been covered. Prior downward re-baselines: #482, #493, #497, the native-app A1 integration paths, and the app-window-selector (ADR 01036) desktop driver paths — appWindows.ts's Windows switch-then-act and macOS window-as-element flows are fully exercised only by the app-windows/app-windows-macos fixtures on the Windows/macOS legs (out-of-process, not under the coverage union); everything hermetically coverable (resolve/close/rect/screenshot branches plus the never-throw degrade paths) has unit tests, leaving only real-driver-only close/raise edges, which drops the union branch rate to ~91.1. 'tolerance' absorbs run-to-run wobble of the non-deterministic E2E union — observed up to ~0.4pp (mostly browser/driver-dependent functions), so it is set to 0.4 and the four values sit below the lowest observed run.
